quick_facts.label: Recruiting stream
quick_facts.value: This is a Non-Technical Popular Categories (NTPC) graduate-level railway post when notified by an RRB. In CEN 01/2019, it was listed as Senior Commercial cum Ticket Clerk under graduate posts.

quick_facts.label: Education requirement
quick_facts.value: The Railway Board cadre order specifies a university degree or equivalent for direct recruitment to Senior Commercial-cum-Ticket Clerk.

quick_facts.label: Pay level
quick_facts.value: The post is in 7th CPC Pay Level 5. The Railway Board cadre order shows the Level-5 pay-matrix range as ₹29,200–₹92,300; actual monthly earnings vary with applicable allowances, posting and government revisions.

quick_facts.label: Medical standard
quick_facts.value: The 2019 RRB NTPC notice assigned the post medical standard B-2. Candidates must meet the medical standards specified in the notification applicable to their recruitment cycle.

quick_facts.label: Direct-recruitment and promotion mix
quick_facts.value: The 2018 Railway Board cadre structure provided for 50% direct recruitment and 50% promotion for Senior Commercial-cum-Ticket Clerks, subject to the governing rules and vacancies.

quick_facts.label: Current-notice caution
quick_facts.value: Vacancies are notification-specific. For example, RRB CEN 05/2024 (NTPC Graduate) listed other graduate NTPC posts and did not list Senior Commercial cum Ticket Clerk; applicants should rely on the latest post-wise RRB notification rather than assume regular annual recruitment.

sections.heading: What the role covers
paragraphs: Senior Commercial cum Ticket Clerks work within Indian Railways’ commercial and ticketing function. The Commercial Department’s remit includes fares and charges, correct collection, accounting and remittance of traffic receipts, passenger amenities, ticket checking, commercial statistics, and reserved and unreserved ticketing systems.

The role-specific 2025 induction curriculum covers coaching and goods work, accounts, safety management, information technology and field training. Related commercial-clerk syllabi cover unreserved-ticketing systems, season tickets, reservations and refunds, concessions, passenger-facing enquiry channels, goods and parcel booking rules, and ticket-checking records. In practice, the exact assignment can therefore vary by station, booking/reservation office, parcel office or other commercial unit.
bullets: Apply ticketing, reservation, refund and fare rules accurately in the assigned commercial function.
Maintain transaction, accountal and commercial records; handle work requiring correct collection and remittance of receipts.
Provide passenger-facing commercial service and use railway IT systems and prescribed procedures.
Undertake the induction and field training required by the railway before or alongside assignment.

sections.heading: Entry through RRB NTPC and internal promotion
paragraphs: For direct entry, watch RRB NTPC notifications for the specific post, verify the post-wise eligibility, apply online to the specified RRB, and follow that notification’s examination and document requirements. In the 2019 NTPC recruitment, Senior Commercial cum Ticket Clerk required a university degree or equivalent, was classified at Level 5 and B-2 medical standard, and had an advertised direct-recruitment age range of 18–33 years as of July 1, 2019. Those dates and limits are historical examples, not standing eligibility rules.

The 2019 notice prescribed two computer-based tests, followed by document verification and medical examination for this post; it did not prescribe a typing or aptitude test for Senior Commercial cum Ticket Clerk. The Railway Board’s 2018 cadre order also provides a promotional channel, with 50% of this grade earmarked for promotion in that cadre structure.
bullets: Complete a university degree or equivalent for direct recruitment when the notified eligibility retains that requirement.
Prepare for the two-stage CBT structure stated in the relevant NTPC notification; syllabus and stage rules can change by CEN.
Meet the notification’s medical standard and present original eligibility documents at document verification.
Serving eligible railway staff may also reach the grade through the applicable departmental promotion process.

sections.heading: Knowledge and skills to build
paragraphs: The official 2025 training module for Senior Commercial cum Ticket Clerks includes foundation training, coaching theory and practical work, goods theory and practical work, accounts, safety management, ethical leadership and soft skills, information technology, establishment matters, field training and an examination. This supports a preparation focus on accuracy, customer communication, rule-based decision-making, digital-system confidence, numerical/accounting discipline and safe working.

For the competitive examination, the 2019 NTPC first-stage CBT used mathematics, general intelligence and reasoning, and general awareness. Candidates should treat a new CEN as authoritative for the current syllabus, number of questions and marking scheme.
bullets: Passenger communication and service recovery within railway rules.
Accurate handling of fares, refunds, records and cash/accountal procedures.
Working knowledge of ticketing, reservation and commercial IT applications.
Numeracy, reasoning and attention to detail for both the CBT and operational records.
Safety awareness, integrity and professional conduct.

sections.heading: Work setting
paragraphs: The post belongs to the Commercial Department, whose functions span passenger-facing commercial services and railway revenue activity, including reserved and unreserved ticketing, passenger amenities, ticket checking and commercial statistics. Accordingly, the work is operational and service-facing rather than a purely back-office clerical job.

Actual location and duty assignment are controlled by the recruiting railway and local administration. Indian Railways’ staff listings show Senior Commercial cum Ticket Clerks assigned under commercial units at stations, including a parcel and luggage office, illustrating that workplace placement can differ across the network.
bullets: Likely work environments include station commercial offices and ticketing, reservation, enquiry, parcel/luggage or related commercial units.
Work requires compliance with centrally prescribed commercial rules while serving local passenger and revenue needs.
Posting location, roster and specific counter/office assignment are not guaranteed by the generic job title.

sections.heading: Career progression context
paragraphs: The Railway Board’s unified commercial-and-ticketing cadre order places Senior Commercial-cum-Ticket Clerk at Level 5. In that structure, the next stated promotion grade is Chief Commercial-cum-Ticket Clerk at Level 6, followed by Commercial Superintendent at Level 7. Progression depends on the applicable selection/non-selection rules, seniority, vacancies, training and departmental conditions rather than occurring automatically.

The same order describes the senior clerk grade as a 50% direct-recruitment and 50% promotion grade, while the Level-6 Chief Commercial-cum-Ticket Clerk and Level-7 Commercial Superintendent grades are shown as promotional grades.
bullets: Level 5: Senior Commercial-cum-Ticket Clerk.
Level 6: Chief Commercial-cum-Ticket Clerk, shown as a promotion-selection grade in the 2018 cadre structure.
Level 7: Commercial Superintendent, shown as a promotion non-selection grade in the 2018 cadre structure.

sections.heading: Pay context
paragraphs: As documented in the 2018 Railway Board cadre order and the 2019 NTPC notice, Senior Commercial cum Ticket Clerk is a 7th CPC Level-5 post with an initial basic pay of ₹29,200 in that recruitment context and a Level-5 pay-matrix range of ₹29,200–₹92,300. This is basic-pay context, not a promise of take-home salary.

Take-home pay is variable: it depends on current central-government pay orders, dearness allowance, house-rent allowance where applicable, transport and other admissible allowances, deductions, and posting. Applicants should check the CEN and current Railway/central-government orders in force on appointment.
bullets: Historical/basic-pay reference: ₹29,200 at Level 5 in the 2019 RRB NTPC notice.
Pay-matrix reference: ₹29,200–₹92,300 in the 2018 cadre order.
Do not equate basic pay with in-hand pay; allowances and deductions change over time.
